model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: This is essentially a coin-flip between two top-5 teams, but Florida gets the slightest nod because its overall efficiency profile is marginally higher on KenPom (AdjEM 33.83 vs. 33.55) and Torvik, with a similarly elite defense and a tiny edge in offensive efficiency. Houston absolutely has a credible path—its defense is just as suffocating and it can win if it controls the glass and turns this into a low-possession grind—but with no confirmed, matchup-flipping availability news in the quick checks, I’ll lean to Florida’s slightly higher two-way baseline and shot-making ceiling.

reasoningStep.title: Elite 8 South Regional Final pick: Florida over Houston
reasoningStep.summary: Florida by a hair in a near-even matchup of two top-5 efficiency teams; confidence is low because Houston’s defensive identity makes the upset very live.
evidence: KenPom: Florida #4 (AdjEM 33.83; AdjO 125.6 #9; AdjD 91.8) vs Houston #5 (AdjEM 33.55; AdjO 124.6 #16; AdjD 91.0).
Torvik: Florida #4 (AdjO rank 11; AdjD rank 5) vs Houston #5 (AdjO rank 13; AdjD rank 4).
Quick ESPN/web scans did not surface a clearly verified, matchup-flipping availability/injury update for either team, so the pick leans on baseline efficiency and matchup fit.
Underdog path acknowledged: Houston can win by controlling tempo/possessions and leveraging its elite defense; the small Florida edge keeps confidence near 50/50.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
